name: Cranberry Nut Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Place hazelnuts in a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 inch jellyroll pan.
3. Bake hazelnuts for 20 minutes, or until toasted. Wrap hot hazelnuts in a clean cloth towel and roll gently back and forth to remove skins. Coarsely chop the hazelnuts.
4. Grease and lightly flour two large cookie sheets.
5. In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
6. In a small bowl, beat 4 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk with a wire whisk or fork (reserve egg white for later use), vanilla, and 1 tablespoon water. Pour the egg mixture into the flour mixture and stir with a wooden spoon. Use hands to knead dough together until very stiff.
7. Knead in hazelnuts and cranberries.
8. Divide dough into four equal pieces. On a lightly floured surface, shape each piece of dough into an 11 x 2-inch log. Place the logs on the cookie sheets about 4 inches apart.
9. With a pastry brush, brush the remaining egg white onto the logs.
10. Place the cookie sheets on two oven racks and bake for 40 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean. Rotate the cookie sheets between upper and lower racks halfway through cooking time. During baking, the logs will spread and become loaf-shaped.
11. Let loaves cool for 10 minutes on the cookie sheet on a rack.
12. Remove loaves to a cutting board. With a bread knife, cut each loaf crosswise into 1/2-inch thick slices. Place the slices cut-side down on the same cookie sheets on the oven racks.
13. Bake the slices for an additional 10 to 15 minutes, or until the Biscotti are lightly toasted. Rotate the cookie sheets between upper and lower racks halfway through baking time.
14. Remove Biscotti to a wire rack. Allow them to cool completely. The Biscotti will harden as they cool. Store in a tightly covered container.
